V-M-002.checks: Identity creation stays deterministic for normal, verified-email, Telegram, and internal users, and Phase-28 registration cutover cannot create duplicate user rows or bypass email ownership proof.
V-M-002.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_verified_registration.py tests/test_telegram_auth.py tests/test_manual_access_policy.py tests/test_manual_client_cli.py tests/test_security.py -v

phase-28-auth-cutover.gate-1: /api/v1/auth/register creates pending registration state only and does not return authenticated user/session material.
phase-28-auth-cutover.gate-2: Verified-email activation creates exactly one canonical user and reuses the existing login token response contract.
phase-28-auth-cutover.gate-3: Existing login, refresh, logout, Telegram auth, and internal-user resolution behavior remains compatible after the registration response shape changes.
phase-28-auth-cutover.gate-4: Duplicate pending requests for the same email cannot create duplicate active users.

scenarios.scenario-1: Verified email activation creates the active user once.
scenarios.scenario-2: Internal user resolved without creating duplicate rows.
scenarios.scenario-3: Refresh and logout contracts remain compatible with M-039 session-security assumptions.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unverified registration cannot authenticate or access protected user profile endpoints.
